The sign Capricorn, represented by a water animal, with the body and tail of a fish, and the forelegs, neck, and head of an antelope, a shark, or alligator. makar kī mandī havā , ( Geog .) The calms of Capricorn. makar manḍal, yā chakkar ; A khat-i-jadī , ( Geog .) The tropic of Capricorn.

ety: Prk. मक्खिआ; S. मक्षिका
def: s.f. A fly; — the sight (of a gun)
more: n. f. S. मक्षिका, , Pālī, makkhikā . 1. A fly. 1. Ab kyā makkhī chhīṅk gaī? (Ironic.). Did a fly sneeze? (What made you drop your work all of a sudden?). 2. Dekhtī ānkkoṅ makkhī nahīṅ niglī jātī . Prov. One does not swallow a fly with his eyes open. (One cannot openly associate with a bad character). 3. Mākhī kin ne chākhī, khoī kis ne dhoī . Rus. (Said by a poor man). Who'd lick a fly, the refuse of the cane who would express? 4. Bahut dāṅt mat nikālo, makkhī lāt mār jāëgī . A jest. A fly will kick you if you grin so. 2. The foresight in a firelock. makkhī-chūs , n. m. lit . one who would suck a fly rather than lose any of the liquor into which it fell. A skinflint; miser. makkhī-mār , n. m. 1. A fly-killer. Makkhī-mār baṛā chamār . Prov. A killer of flies, a cobbler plies. 2. A dirty, filthy fellow. 3. One who exactly copies every mistake, blot, etc. of the original. makkhī-mārnā , v. …

s. f. 1. A fly. 2. The sight of a gun. مکهي اڙانا makkhī uṛānā, 1. To flatter or perform servile offices for any one. 2. To have ulcers on the body (so as to be constantly employed in driving away the flies from them). 3. To be a good marksman (so as to hit a fly with a ball or arrow). مکهي جهلنا makkhī jhalnā, To have venereal ulcers. مکهي چوس makkhī-chūs, A miser, a niggard, a skin-flint. مکهي مارنا makkhī mārnā, To idle, to be unemployed, to trifle, to fool.
subs: (or makkhiyāṅ) uṛānā or jhalnā (-par-se), To drive away flies (from off); — to perform servile offices (for); to flatter, to fawn (upon); — to have ulcers on the body (so as to be constantly employed in driving away the flies from them); — to fritter away the time, to trifle, to idle
(-par-se), To knock off a fly (from a thing or place, with a ball or an arrow), to be a good marksman
s.m. lit. 'One who would suck a fly rather than lose any of the food or drink into which it fell'; a miser, niggard, skinflint
s.m. One who kills a fly or flies; — a dirty, fllthy fellow; an idler, a lazy fellow; — one who makes an exact copy of a document, &c., mistakes, and blots, &c. included
To kill flies; to be unemployed, to idle, to trifle, to fool (syn. makkhī uṛānā)
(-par), Flies to buzz (about), or to sit (upon); — to be disgusting, or ugly, &c.
To swallow a living fly; — to bring evil upon oneself; — to tell great lies
Not to allow a fly to sit on the nose; — to shrink from incurring an obligation.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀺𑀆 (makkhiā), from Sanskrit मक्षिका (makṣikā). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੱਖੀ (makkhī).

Actions which the Mah. law condemns as wrong, but does not invalidate.

(from قصد) s. m. Intention, design, meaning, purpose, purport, object, aim, scope, desire, wish. (Plur. مقاصد).
subs: or maqs̤ad honā (-kā, or merā), (One's) object to be accomplished or attained; (one's) wish to be gained
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَقْصَد (maqsad), borrowed from Arabic مَقْصَد (maqṣad).

ety: v.n. fr. قسم 'to divide,' &c.
def: s.m. Part, portion.
more: (from قسم Heb. and Chald. ) s. m. A part, a portion. ¶ maḳsim (noun of place) A place of partition. ¶ muḳsim, part. act. (IV.) Swearing (by God). ¶ muḳsam (part. pass. IV.) s. m. 1. An oath. 2. A place where they swear. ¶ muḳassim, part. act. (II.) Dividing, distributing, dispersing. ¶ muḳassam (part. pass. II.) adj. 1. Divided. 2. Having a beautiful (face). 3. Beautified.

def: s.m. Hypocrisy; — wrath; — multitude.
s.f. (rustic or dialec.) = makkhī, q.v. s.f. A fly; — the sight (of a gun)
subs: (or makkhiyāṅ) uṛānā or jhalnā (-par-se), To drive away flies (from off); — to perform servile offices (for); to flatter, to fawn (upon); — to have ulcers on the body (so as to be constantly employed in driving away the flies from them); — to fritter away the time, to trifle, to idle
(-par-se), To knock off a fly (from a thing or place, with a ball or an arrow), to be a good marksman
s.m. lit. 'One who would suck a fly rather than lose any of the food or drink into which it fell'; a miser, niggard, skinflint
s.m. One who kills a fly or flies; — a dirty, fllthy fellow; an idler, a lazy fellow; — one who makes an exact copy of a document, &c., mistakes, and blots, &c. included
To kill flies; to be unemployed, to idle, to trifle, to fool (syn. makkhī uṛānā)
(-par), Flies to buzz (about), or to sit (upon); — to be disgusting, or ugly, &c.
To swallow a living fly; — to bring evil upon oneself; — to tell great lies
Not to allow a fly to sit on the nose; — to shrink from incurring an obligation.

def: s.f. A fly; a bee (i.q. makkhī, q.v.). s.f. A fly; — the sight (of a gun)
subs: (or makkhiyāṅ) uṛānā or jhalnā (-par-se), To drive away flies (from off); — to perform servile offices (for); to flatter, to fawn (upon); — to have ulcers on the body (so as to be constantly employed in driving away the flies from them); — to fritter away the time, to trifle, to idle
(-par-se), To knock off a fly (from a thing or place, with a ball or an arrow), to be a good marksman
s.m. lit. 'One who would suck a fly rather than lose any of the food or drink into which it fell'; a miser, niggard, skinflint
s.m. One who kills a fly or flies; — a dirty, fllthy fellow; an idler, a lazy fellow; — one who makes an exact copy of a document, &c., mistakes, and blots, &c. included
To kill flies; to be unemployed, to idle, to trifle, to fool (syn. makkhī uṛānā)
(-par), Flies to buzz (about), or to sit (upon); — to be disgusting, or ugly, &c.
To swallow a living fly; — to bring evil upon oneself; — to tell great lies
Not to allow a fly to sit on the nose; — to shrink from incurring an obligation.

def: s.f. = مکشکا makshikā, q.v. s.m. Honey; — a kind of honey-like mineral substance or pyrites (of which there are various kinds)
subs: vulg. mākshikaj, s.m. Bees'-wax (lit. 'produced from honey').

def: s.m. Honey; — a kind of honey-like mineral substance or pyrites (of which there are various kinds)
subs: vulg. mākshikaj, s.m. Bees'-wax (lit. 'produced from honey').

ety: pass. part. of کشف 'to uncover,' &c.
def: part. adj. Uncovered; discovered, detected; disclosed; displayed; revealed; — open.
more: (part. pass. of کشف Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Revealed, disclosed, open, detected, discovered.

ety: n. of place fr. قسم 'to divide,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of partition.

ety: for A. مکسوبة maksūbat, fem. of maksūb, pass. part. of کسب 'to acquire,' &c.
def: part. adj. Acquired, gained, gotten, earned.

ety: pass. part. of قصد 'to intend, desire,' &c.
def: part. adj. Intended, meant; purposed, designed; proposed; desired, wished, sought; — s.m. Intent, intention, design, purpose, drift, aim, view, desire, object, scope (syn. maqṣad).
more: part. pass. (of قصد) Intended, proposed; s. m. Intent, &c. See مقصد maḳṣad.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَقْصُود (maqsūd), from Arabic مَقْصُود (maqṣūd).

ety: pass. part. of قسم 'to divide,' &c.
def: part. adj. Divided, distributed, portioned out, apportioned, allotted; — s.m. Portion, share; allotment; lot, fate, destiny; (in Arith.) the dividend
more: adj. قسم dividing. Divided; partitioned. maqsūm , n. m. 1. A portion; share; allotment; lot. 2. Fate; destiny. Maqsūm kī bāt hai . 3. (H. baṭā āṅk ; S. भाज्य ) ( Arith .) The dividend. maqsūm kā likkhā , What is written in one's fate; predestination. maqsūm-alah ; H. bāṭne-vālā ; S. भाजक ( Arith .) The divisor. maqsūm-alah āzam, ād-i-āzam ; H. baṛā bāṭne-vālā ; S. महतमापवर्त्तक ( Arith .) The greatest common measure, G. C. M.
(part. pass. of قسم) adj. Distributed, allotted, divided; s. m. Portion, lot.
subs: s.m. Createst common divisor or factor, the G.C.M.
It is a matter of fate or destiny.

ety: pass. part. of کسر 'to break,' &c.
def: part. adj. Broken; cut; — marked with the vowel kasra (a consonant); — s.m. (in Arith.) A fraction; — a consonant marked with the vowel kasra (ِ).
more: n. m. کسر A letter marked with the vowel kasra or zer ( ِ )
(part. pass. of کسر) adj. 1. A letter marked with the vowel kasra (ِ). 2. Broken, cut. 3. A fraction (in arithmetick).
ex: first letter [is followed by a] kasra
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مکسور (maksūr), from Arabic مَكْسُور (maksūr).

ety: for A. مقصوره maqṣūrat, fem. of maqṣūr, pass. part. of قصر 'to confine, restrict,' &c.
def: part. adj. Confined, restricted; — abridged, abbreviated, curtailed, diminished; — s.m. (for alif-ě-maqṣūra), The abbreviated or short alif (as in دنييٰٰ or دنيا dunyā).
more: adj. قصر 1. Abridged. 2. Abbreviated. alif maqsūrah , The short a .
(part. pass. fem. of قصر) Restricted, abbreviated, diminished.

ety: for A. مکسورة maksūrat, fem. of maksūr
def: part. adj. = maksūr.

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, of قطع 'to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. Cutting, severing, dividing; amputation; a cut; — interruption, an abrupt breaking off; a place of separation: a pause (in reading verse), a caesura; — the last verse of a poem (in which the writer introduces his nom de plume)
more: n. m. قطع The last line of a poem in which the writer introduces his nom de plume. maqte kā band , The chorus of a song.

(noun of place or inf. from قطع Chald. ) s. m. 1. The last verse of a poem. 2. Cutting, amputating. ¶ miḳt̤aﻌ (noun of instrum.) Shears for cutting cloth, silver, &c. a cutting instrument in general. ¶ muḳat̤t̤aﻌ (part. pass. II.) adj. 1. Cut, cut out, shaped. 2. Well dressed, well shaped, beautiful.
subs: (vulg. maqte-kā) band, Chorus of a song.
wety: Borrowed from Persian مَقْطَع (maqta').

r: maktab, madrasa, maktab baiṭhānā, baiṭhā-denā, meṅ, maktab-paṛhānā, maktab-ḵẖāna
ety: n. of place fr. کتب 'to write'
def: s.m. School-house, school; — a gambling-house
more: n. m. کتب 1. A place for teaching writing, as distinguished from a madrasah , q. v. 2. A Mah. school. 3. A feast given by the parents when a boy is first sent to school. maktab paṛhānā , v. a. To teach in a school. mak'tab-khānah , n. m. 1. A house; school. 2. ( Gamblers ) A gambling house. maktab kā yār , A school-mate or fellow. maktab ke laṛke , Schoolboys.
(noun of place of کتب Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A school, academy. Also مکتب خانه maktab-ḵẖāna, A school.
subs: s.m. pl. The boys of a school; school-boys.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَکْتَب (maktab), froml Arabic مَكْتَب (maktab).

ety: n. of place fr. قتل 'to kill'
def: s.m. Place of slaughter, or of execution; — place of death, any part of the body which, being wounded, causes death; mortal spot, vital part (syn. maram).
more: (noun of place of قتل Heb. and Aram. ) 1. A place of slaughter or of execution. 2. The place of death, i. e. any diseased or wounded part of the body that causes death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَقْتَل (maqtal), itself from Arabic مَقْتَل (maqtal). First attested as Middle Hindi مَقْتَل (maqtal) in 1503 A.D.

ety: pass. part. of کتب 'to write'
def: part. adj. Written; — sewn or stitched, or pasted, together; — s.m. What is written; a writing; a letter, an epistle; — a collection of letters (generally pasted together) forming a long roll
more: n. m. کتب wrote. 1. A writing; letter ( chiṭṭhī , 1). 2. A collection of letters generally pasted one to the other, forming a long roll. Maktūb paṛhā karo! khat paṛhnā ā jāëgā. maktūb-ilah , n. m. The addressee; drawee.
(part. pass. of کتب) 1. adj. Written. 2. s. m. A letter, epistle. (Plur. مکاتيب makātīb and مکتوبات maktūbāt ).
subs: s.m. Person written to; addressee; drawee.

def: s.m. pl. (of maktūb), Writings; letters (syn. makātīb).

ety: fr. maktūb
def: adj. Epistolary; — delivered in writing.

ety: pass. part. of قتل 'to kill'
def: part. Killed, slain, slaughtered; — s.m. One who is slain; — a lover
more: adj. Killed; slain. maqtūl , n. m. ( Poet .) A lover. maqtūl o majrūh . Killed and wounded.
(part. pass. of قتل) adj. Killed, slain.

ety: fr. maqtūl
def: s.f. The being killed or slain.
more: s. f. The being killed, killing.

ety: pass. part. of کتم 'to hide, conceal'
def: part. adj. Hidden, concealed.

ety: prob. Prk. मक्खु; S. मक्षु
def: adv. (dialec.) = baran, or varam, qq.v. s.m. Rain; rainy season.

ety: pass. part. of عقل 'to be or become intelligent,' &c.
def: adj. Perceived by the intellect, intellectual; excogitated (opp. to manqūl, 'desumed,' or 'borrowed,' as is the science of the fundamentals of religion, and the like); — intelligible; perceptible; — understood; — approved by the intellect; approved; acceptable; reasonable; probable; just, right, proper; fair; decent; liberal; sufficient; satisfactory; — pertinent, apposite, appropriate, to the purpose; — formed in wisdom; good, nice, choice; — s.f. Any branch of knowledge that is excogitated (the opp. of manqūl, see above); philosophy, &c.; — intj. Right! just! all right. very good! very well! &c.
more: adj. عقل See اچهل 5—15, 13, 17—20, and ṭhīk 4—7, 15. 1. Reasonable. 2. Right. 3. Fair. 4. Good and sufficient; satisfactory. 5. Worthy. 6. Acceptable. 7. Appropriate. 8. Pertinent. 9. Expedient. 10. Liberal; good. māqūl , n. f. Pl. māqūlāt . Philosophy. Māqūl paṛhte ho yā manqūl? māqūl! bajā! intj. All right ( durust )! very good! very well! māqūl tankhuāh , A liberal salary.

(part. pass. of عقل Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Intelligible, reasonable, probable, just, pertinent, proper, fair, decent. Plur. معقولات maﻌḳūlāt, Probabilities, words or things consistent with reason. معقول بين maﻌḳūl-bīn, Judicious, reasonable. معقول گو maﻌḳūl-go, Speaking properly, conversable. معقول گوئي maﻌḳūl-go-ī, s. f. Pertinent or proper speaking.
subs: adj. & s.m. Seeing (or one who sees) what is acceptable, or reasonable, &c.; judicious; reasonable; — a judicious, or a reasonable, person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Speaking (or one who speaks) reasonably, or aptly, or properly, &c.; conversable
s.f. Pertinent, or proper, speaking or speech.

ety: pass. part. of أکل
def: part. Eaten, devoured; — s.m. What is eaten, food, meat, victuals.

ety: for A. معقولة maʻqūlat, fem. of maʻqūl
def: part. adj. = maʻqūl, q.v.; — s.m. That which is understood, &c.; the understood, the intelligible.
more: s. m. The understood, the intelligible.

ety: for A. مقولة maqūlat, fem. of maqūl, pass. part. of قال (for قول
def: part. adj. & s.m. Said; repeated over and over again; — what is said, a word; a saying; an adage, a proverb, a maxim, an aphorism; — a quotation
more: n. m. قول 1. A saying; an adage ( maslah ). 2. A quotation. maqūlah bayān k. , v. a. To quote.
(part. pass. fem. of قول) adj. Said, repeated over and over again. s. m. A maxim, saying, adage, proverb, aphorism.
subs: (-kā), To mention or repeat a saying (of), to quote (from).

def: s.m. pl. (of mākūl), Eatables, victuals, viands.
s.m. pl. (of maʻqūl), Things perceived by the intellect, intellectual things; intelligible things; — things approved by the intellect, reasonable things, or words; probabilities, &c.
s.m. pl. of maqūla, q.v.

ety: maʻqūl, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Reasonableness; probability, &c. (see maʻqūl).

ety: for A. معقولية maʻqūlīyat, abst. s. fr. maʻqūl
def: s.f. Reasonableness; probability, &c. (see maʻqūl).

ety: pass. part. of عکس 'to invert, reverse; to transpose,' &c.
def: part. adj. Inverted; reversed; turned upside down; converted; transposed; — reflex; — perverted (in order, or in meaning)
more: adj. عکس Inverted; reversed; reflex. mākūs paṛhnā , To read (the Qurān) backwards. Repeating certain incantations, as saying one's prayers backwards was, with us, an appropriate step to be taken when about to invoke the aid of the powers of darkness.
(part. pass. of عکس) adj. Inverted, reversed, reflex.
subs: v.n. To be or become inverted, or reversed, or turned upside down.

def: s.m. Riches, money, property, wealth, possessions, merchandise, stock, goods, effects, estate; rent or revenue (from land); — finance; — a prize (in a lottery, &c.); any great thing, a something, a thing of importance or consequence (e.g. wǒh kyā māl hai ki maiṅ usko girā na sakūṅgā); a dainty, tit-bit; — square (of a number, e.g. 9 is the māl of 3); — indigo dye, manufactured indigo, or the plant in the leaf and bundle (opp. to pāb), the granulated sediment in an indigo vat after heating and drawing off the water
more: n. m. See اسباب (3—7). 1. ( māl ṭāl ) Property; money. Māl par zakāt hai . Prov. On property poor rates. 2. ( māl matāa ) Goods. 3. Revenue; finance. 4. A prize (in a lottery, etc.). 5. Dainties; tit bits. 6. A square. 7. The granulated sediment in an Indigo vat after heating and drawing off the water. 8. ( P. O. Slang ) The mail bags. māl uṛānā , v. n. To feed on dainties. māl-ul-māl ; S. चतुर्घात; H. chauthā chaṛhāo . ( Math .) The fourth power. mālā māl , adj. Full; abundant ( bhar-pūr ). mālā māl k. , v. a. 1. See bhar-pūr k . 2. To enrich. māl amvāt , n. m. 1. Property left by the deceased. 2. Unappropriated or unclaimed property. māl bar-āmad , n. m. 1. Export. 2. ( māl bar-āmdah ) Recovered property. 3. ( māl guzāshta-i-mujrim ) Property left by a criminal. māl bar-āmad aur dar-āmad kī vusūl bāqī . Balance of trade. māl be-khaṭke . Unencumbered property. māl chīrnā , v. a. To embe…
n. f. 1. The string of a spinning wheel or reel. 2. ( Mech .) Belting.

s. m. 1. Property, wealth, merchandise, goods, effects. 2. A prize (in a lottery, &c.). مال خاوند māl-ḵẖāwind, A proprietor. مالدار māl-dār, adj. Wealthy, rich. مالداري māl-dār-ī, s. f. Wealthiness. مال زاده māl-zāda, A son of a whore, a pimp or pander. مال زادي māl-zādī, s. f. A bawd, a whore. مالضامن māl-ẓāmin, A security (for property, or money, in opposition to حاضرضامن ḥāẓir-ẓāmin ). مال مردم خور māl-i-mardum-ḵẖor, A consumer of the property of others; one who borrows and never pays. مال مست māl-mast, adj. Purse-proud.
s. m. A wrestler, boxer, prizefighter.
s. f. Garland, &c. see مالا mālā.
part. act. (from ماليدن) in compos. Rubbing, rubber; napkin, towel.
subs: To squander wealth, &c.; — to feed on dainties
s.m. The square of a square, the fourth power
adj. Heaped, brimful; full, replete, abundant; — māl-ā-māl karnā, v.t. To heap up; to fill to the brim; — to enrich
s.m. Property left by a deceased person; unappropriated or unclaimed property
s.m. Export; — recovered property ( = māl bar-āmada); — property left by a criminal ( = māl guẕāshtaʼě mujrim)
Balance of exports and imports, balance of trade
vulg. māl-ḵẖāwind, s.m. A man of property; a proprietor
adj. & s.m. Wealthy, rich, moneyed; — a wealthy person; a proprietor
s.m. A wealthy man; a proprietor
s.m. A son of a whore, a pimp or pander
s.m. Misoellaneous revenues (from customs and other sources, exclusive of land); duties
s.m. Joint or undivided property; joint stock
s.m. A person who stands security (for property, or money, in opp. to ḥāẓir-ẓāmin), a surety, bondsman (for the payment of rent or a debt)
s.f. Security (for rent or debt); pecuniary bail; security in property
Property to be (cut off, or) stolen; — goods to be sold in large quantities (baṛā being generally expressed, e.g. wahāṅ baṛā māl kaṭtā hai)
s.f. Goods'-van, luggage-van; — luggage (or goods') train
adj. & s.m. Paying rent or taxes; — one who pays revenue to (or holds land under) government, a landholder, landlord, a manager of cultivation and village matters, a tenant, a subject
s.f. Land-revenue; rent paid in money; — the paying of rent and taxes; — the state of a renter; — assessed land
or māl-ě-maurūs̤a, s.m. A bequest, legacy
s.m. 'Criminal and property'; a criminal with property
s.m. A consumer of the property of others; a swindler; one who embezzles; one who borrows and never pays
adj. Intoxicated with wealth, purse-proud, boastful
(or muft-kā māl), s.m. Property acquired without cost or labour
s.m. Property devoted to religious purposes
Unclaimed property; — anything bought cheap or got for nothing
s.m. A valuable prize; a dainty, tit-bit, delicacy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: Prk. मल्लो; S. मल्लः
def: s.m. A wrestler, professional boxer, prize-fighter; an athlete, a champion, a hero (syn. pahlawān).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: Prk. मल्लं; S. माल्यं; — or S. माला
def: s.f. The staff-tree, Celastrus paniculatus, or C. nutans (the aromatic bitter seeds yield an empyreumatic oil (Oleum nigrum) which is used in lamps; and in medicine, having been found a successful remedy in beriberi, rheumatism, and paralysis, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: rt. of mālīdan; — rt. Zend mared = S. मर्द् (मृद्)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Wiping, rubbing; twisting; crushing; — rubber, wiper, napkin, towel (used as last member of compounds; e.g. gosh-māl, rū-māl, &c., qq.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: S. मलं
def: s.m. Dirt, filth; dust; impurity; impure matter; excrement; refuse; sediment; dregs; scum; alloy or dross (of metal); rust; — defilement, moral impurity, sin; — any excretion of the body, e.g. adeps, marrow, serum, semen, blood, fæces, mucus (of the nose), wax (of the ear), sweat, rheum, phlegm, tears, dandruff; — camphor; cuttle-fish bone
more: n. m. Filth; excrement. mal mūtr , n. m. Excrements and urine.
n. m. S. मल्ल An affix to Hin. names. mal'jī , n. m. 1. A form of address to a Hindu gentleman. 2. A merryandrew.
n. m. S. मल्ल. 1. A professional wrestler. 2. An athlete ( pahalvān ). mal-yudh , n. m. Wrestling ( kushtī ).
s. m. 1. Dirt. 2. Excrement. मल्ल mall, s. m. A wrestler, a boxer. مل يدهہ मल्लयुद्ध malla-yuddha, Wrestling.
subs: s.m. A piece of cloth worn over the privities
or mal-hārī, adj. Filth-removing; taking away impurity, &c.
s.f. The removal or destruction of filth, or impurity, &c.

def: horse and cattle, cattle (in general)
wety: From مال (māl) + مویشی (maveśī).

def: s.f. A wreath, garland, chaplet (of flowers, &c.); — a row, line; a chain, string (of beads, &c.), a necklace, a Hindū rosary; a collection, group, a collection of words arranged in order, a vocabulary, a dictionary; a book; — a field; — a cocoa-nut shell
more: n. f. S. माला, Pr. mālāu, malāo . 1. A necklace; garland. 2. A Hindu rosary; a chaplet of beads. mālā phernā, yā japnā , v. a. To tell one's beads. 1. Pherūṅ tasbī na japūṅ mālā, Nā murdār, na karūṅ halālā . Kabīr. Nor Moslem's beads I tell, nor Hin dus e'er, No flesh I eat, killed in what way soe'er. 2. Aiṅḍī baiṅḍī dhotī bāṅdhe, lambī mālā japtā hai, Man meṅ kapaṭ, tere hāth katarnī, yūṅ kyā Ishvar miltā hai ? Kabīr. In sinuous strip of cloth snake-coiled, Counting long string of beads, and moiled, Guile in thy heart, in hand a knife, Is God then found, O man, with strife?
s. f. 1. A Hindū rosary. 2. A necklace, a garland. 3. A book.
subs: s.m. The seed of the Elœocarpus ganitrus (of which rosaries are made)
or mālā japnā, To tell (one's) beads
s.m. (f. -ī), A wreath-maker, garland-weaver; a florist, gardener, flower-seller
adj. (f. -vatī), Having a wreath or garland, garlanded, wreathed, crowned.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit माला (mālā).

ety: perf. part. of malnā; — and = Prk. मलिअओ; S. मृदित+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Rubbed, scrubbed, &c.; worn; laid bare; effaced
subs: adj. & s.m. & f. 'With the five marks effaced,' turned five years (a horse); over eight, or ten, years; aged; — a horse over five years, &c. (cf. dale-panj).

def: s.m. Name of a district lying west and south-west of Bengal; — name of a barbarous tribe of people; a man of that tribe; — an epithet of Vishṇu.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مال (māl), from Arabic مَال (māl).

ety: for A. malʼā, fem. of malʼānu, v.n. fr. مَلِئَي 'to be full'
def: adj. Full.

ety: see malewār
def: s.m. The country Malabar

ety: S. मलय+गिरिः
def: s.m. The Malaya mountains (or a mountain in that range) in Malabar (answering to the western Ghāṭs; it is from this range that the best sandal-wood is brought).
more: s. m. A mountain or mountainous range, from which the best sandal-wood is brought, answering to the western Ghāṭs in the peninsula of India.

def: s.m. = malāgir, q.v.; — 2˚ adj. & s.m. = malagīrī, q.v. s.m. The Malaya mountains (or a mountain in that range) in Malabar (answering to the western Ghāṭs; it is from this range that the best sandal-wood is brought).
more: n. m. S. मलयगिरिज 1. The best sandal-wood. 2. A hill bird.

ety: malāgīr, q.v.+ī = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Of the colour of sandal-wood; — the colour of sandal-wood; — the best sandal-wood; — name of a hill-bird.
more: adj. Of the color of sandal wood.
s. m. The colour of sandal-wood.

def: s.m. A bulrush.

ety: for A. ملاحة, inf. n. of ملح 'to be salt'
def: s.f. The being salt, or savoury; the being good, agreeable, or elegant; goodness; elegance; beauty; excellence; fineness; delicacy; — beauty of the mouth; — piquant look or appearance; a rich brown (or dark) complexion.
more: n. f. ملح A rich brown complexion.
(from ملح) s. f. 1. Being salt. 2. Elegancy. 3. Beauty, excellence.

def: s.m. pl. (of mulḥid), Heretics; infidels, atheists.
more: s. plur. (of ملحد) Infidels, atheists, hereticks.

ety: prob. Prk. मंडअइआ; S. मण्ड+क+इका
def: s.f. Scum (of soup, or boiled rice, or of any liquid); cream; clotted cream; — a kind of dish made of milk
more: n. f. S. मण्ड Cream; clotted cream.
n. f. Rubbing down a horse, etc.

ety: malā(nā)+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Rubbing; scouring; rubbing down (a horse, &c.); — price paid for rubbing, or scrubbing, &c.

ety: prob. mal(nā)+Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य + ता + इका
def: s.f. Rich alluvial soil on the bank of a river; meadow-land.
more: n. Rich alluvial ground along the banks of rivers; meadow land.

def: s. plur. (of ملک malak, Heb. ) Angels.
(pl. of ملك malak), = P ملائکه malāʼika (for A. ملائکة malāʼikat), s.m. pl. Angels.
s. plur. (of ملک malak, Heb. ) Angels.
(for A. ملائکة malāʼikat), = A ملائِك malāʼik (pl. of ملك malak), s.m. pl. Angels.
s.m. pl. (of malʻūn), The cursed, or accursed.
more: s. plur. of ملعون malʼūﻌn, q. v.

def: Malaysia (a country in Southeast Asia)

def: s.f. A garland.
s.f. A garland; a necklace, &c. ( = mālā, q.v.); a row, series; a multitude; — the double jasmine; — an intoxicating drink; — a daughter; — a palace.
s.f. A female messenger, a confidante; — an amorous woman, a lewd woman; — a female elephant.
s.m. = malāḵẖā, q.v. s.m. Malacca; — adj. Malay, Malayan.

ety: for A. ملکة malakat, inf. n. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.
def: s.m. Possession; — an acquired habit or principle; — quickness of apprehension or perception; strength of intellect; intellect, genius; — proficiency; mastery.
more: n. f. 1. A queen. 2. ( malkā masūr ) The pulse masūr , q. v. malkah moazzamah , The exalted queen. malkah Vikṭūriā , n. f. Queen Victoria.
n. m. 1. Quickness of perception; intellect; genius. 2. Proficiency; mastery.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ملکه (malika), from Arabic مَلِكَة (malika), feminine of مَلِك (malik, “king”).

def: s.f. pl. (of ملکة malakat, inf. n. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.), Possessions; qualities, endowments; habits, principles.
s.f. contrac. of malkāhaṭ, q.v.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Doubt, apprehension: see وسواس waswās.

def: s.m. A locust (syn. ṭiḍḍī).

ety: P. ملاخه malāḵẖa
def: s.m. Malacca; — adj. Malay, Malayan.

ety: rel. n. fr. malak, q.v.
def: adj. Angelic
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ملکی (mulkī), from Arabic مُلْكِيّ (mulkiyy). Equivalent to مُلْک (mulk, “country”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: for A. ملکية malakīyat, fem. of malakī
def: adj. = malakī, q.v. adj. Angelic

ety: malak˚, prob. = Prk. मलक्क(इ), or मलक्के(इ), fr. S. मल+कृ
def: v.n. (dialec.) To feel nausea, to be sick at stomach; to be bilious; — to be troubled with doubt, or suspicion; to be apprehensive (of).
more: v. n. To perk oneself; to strut ( maṭaknā ).
v. n. 1. To walk like a chairman, &c. (trotting and raising the shoulders). 2. (dakh.) To feel a nausea, to be sick at stomach: see متلانا جيکا.

ety: cf. maṭaknā
def: v.n. To walk like a kahār or chairman, &c. (trotting and raising the shoulders); — to walk affectedly, to perk oneself, to strut.

ety: malak, q.v.+ūt, a termn. borrowed fr. the Aramaic
def: s.f. Kingdom, empire, dominion; — (for ʻālam-ě-malakūt), the world of angels or spirits; the heavenly court; — angels; spirits.
more: (Heb. ) s. f. 1. Spirits, angels, the world of angels. 2. Empire, dominion.

ety: inf. n. of ملّ 'to be tired,' &c.
def: s.m. Weariness, fatigue, languor, ennui; dejection, sadness, melancholy; affliction, grief, anguish; vexation, displeasure
more: (from ملل) s. m. Sadness, grief, anguish, languor, vexation, dejection (of mind).
subs: (-ko), Languor, &c. to come (upon); to be seized with sadness, &c.

ety: for A. ملالة, inf. n. of ملّ 'to be weary,' &c.
def: s.f. = malāl, q.v. s.m. Weariness, fatigue, languor, ennui; dejection, sadness, melancholy; affliction, grief, anguish; vexation, displeasure
more: (inf. from ملل) s. f. Tiresomeness, vexation, sadness, dejectedness.
subs: (-ko), Languor, &c. to come (upon); to be seized with sadness, &c.

r: malām, malāmat, malāmat karnā, par, ko
def: (inf. n. of لام (for لوم) 'to reprove,' &c.), s.m. = P ملامت malāmat (for A. ملامة, inf. n. of لام 'to reprove'), s.f. Reproof, rebuke, censure, reprehension, reproach, accusation, blame; reviling; disgrace; opprobrium; contumely
more: (from لوم) s. m. Reproach, reprehension, rebuke, blame, accusation, reviling.

ety: māl + ā + māl
def: adj. See s.v. māl, 'wealth.' s.m. Riches, money, property, wealth, possessions, merchandise, stock, goods, effects, estate; rent or revenue (from land); — finance; — a prize (in a lottery, &c.); any great thing, a something, a thing of importance or consequence (e.g. wǒh kyā māl hai ki maiṅ usko girā na sakūṅgā); a dainty, tit-bit; — square (of a number, e.g. 9 is the māl of 3); — indigo dye, manufactured indigo, or the plant in the leaf and bundle (opp. to pāb), the granulated sediment in an indigo vat after heating and drawing off the water
subs: To squander wealth, &c.; — to feed on dainties
s.m. The square of a square, the fourth power
adj. Heaped, brimful; full, replete, abundant; — māl-ā-māl karnā, v.t. To heap up; to fill to the brim; — to enrich
s.m. Property left by a deceased person; unappropriated or unclaimed property
s.m. Export; — recovered property ( = māl bar-āmada); — property left by a criminal ( = māl guẕāshtaʼě mujrim)
Balance of exports and imports, balance of trade
vulg. māl-ḵẖāwind, s.m. A man of property; a proprietor
adj. & s.m. Wealthy, rich, moneyed; — a wealthy person; a proprietor
s.m. A wealthy man; a proprietor
s.m. A son of a whore, a pimp or pander
s.m. Misoellaneous revenues (from customs and other sources, exclusive of land); duties
s.m. Joint or undivided property; joint stock
s.m. A person who stands security (for property, or money, in opp. to ḥāẓir-ẓāmin), a surety, bondsman (for the payment of rent or a debt)
s.f. Security (for rent or debt); pecuniary bail; security in property
Property to be (cut off, or) stolen; — goods to be sold in large quantities (baṛā being generally expressed, e.g. wahāṅ baṛā māl kaṭtā hai)
s.f. Goods'-van, luggage-van; — luggage (or goods') train
adj. & s.m. Paying rent or taxes; — one who pays revenue to (or holds land under) government, a landholder, landlord, a manager of cultivation and village matters, a tenant, a subject
s.f. Land-revenue; rent paid in money; — the paying of rent and taxes; — the state of a renter; — assessed land
or māl-ě-maurūs̤a, s.m. A bequest, legacy
s.m. 'Criminal and property'; a criminal with property
s.m. A consumer of the property of others; a swindler; one who embezzles; one who borrows and never pays
adj. Intoxicated with wealth, purse-proud, boastful
(or muft-kā māl), s.m. Property acquired without cost or labour
s.m. Property devoted to religious purposes
Unclaimed property; — anything bought cheap or got for nothing
s.m. A valuable prize; a dainty, tit-bit, delicacy.

ety: fr. malāmat

wety: Causative of ملنا (milnā).

ety: P. also malang
def: s.m. A kind of Moḥammadan derwish, who lets the hair of his head grow to its full length, and leaves it uncombed and dishevelled; — a derwish in a state of ecstasy; a religious enthusiast; — a careless or inconsiderate person; — a tall, robust fellow; — a kind of bird.
more: n. m. S. महोत्तुङ्ग 1. A tall robust fellow. 2. A Mah. mendicant with long unkempt hair. 3. A careless or inconsiderate person. 4. A bird.
s. m. 1. A kind of dervis. 2. Name of a bird.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ملنگ (malang, “jovial, lively, ecstasied, tipsy”).

ety: P. also malang

s.m. A labourer employed in manufacturing salt, a salt-maker.

(from ملنا malnā ) s. m. A worn rupee or other coin.

def: s.f. A bud, blossom; — a maid, virgin, young woman; — moonshine, moonlight; night; — the name of several plants and flowers, e.g. Jasminum grandiflorum; Bignonia suaveolens; Echites caryophyllata; Gaertnera racemosa, &c.
more: n. f. A shrub; Aganosma roxburghii , Wat.
s. f. Name given to different species of shrubs. 1. Echites caryophyllata. 2. Jasminum grandiflorum. 3. (or مالتي لتا māltī-latā ) Gærtnera racemosa.

def: s.m. The country of Malwa (in Central India); — the inhabitants of Malwa

def: s.f. The plant Ipomæa (or Convolvulus) turpethum (com. called teʼorī).

ety: malayā = S. मलय+कः
def: s.m. Name of a mountain, or of a mountainous range, in Malabar; the western Ghāṭs in the Dekkan, or peninsula of India (from these mountains the finest sandal-wood is brought); — name of the country (Malayālam or Malabār) which lies to the east of the Malaya range; — the garden of Indra; a celestial grove; — (S. malayās), name of one of the minor Dvīpas or divisions of the world
more: s. m. Name of a mountain. See ملاگر malāgir.
subs: vulg. malayaj, s.m. lit. 'Growing on the Malaya mountains'; a sandal-tree; sandal-wood
(˚ya+ać), s.m. The Malaya mountains, answering to the western Ghāṭs
or malayā-giri, or malayā-gīr, s.m. = malayāćal, q.v.

more: (noun of place of لوذ Heb. ) s. m. An asylum, refuge, protection.

ety: Prk. मलेअव्वअं; S. मर्दितव्य+कं, rt. मृद्; or fr. S. मल
def: s.m. The rubbish of a fallen building, or wall, &c.; the stones, brick, mortar, &c. lying jumbled together; — rubbish, litter, refuse, sweepings, dirt
more: n. m. H. mail dirt. 1. Rubbish; dirt; sweepings. 2. Bricks, stones, earth, etc. of a broken house. 3. Village expenses.

def: s.m. pl. (of malbūs), Clothes, garments.

ety: fr. malbūs
def: adj. (dialec.) Worn before; usually put on, in ordinary wear.

ety: fr. malnā-dalnā, q.v.s.v. malnā
def: s.m. Rubbing; grinding; pressing, squeezing
subs: v.t. To rub; grind; press, squeeze; — to rub down (a horse, &c.); to thresh (corn, &c.); to beat.

def: adj. Wealthy (دولتمند). māl-dār, māl-vālā , n. m. A wealthy person.

n. f. Opulence (دولتمندي). māl-dār-i-amānat . A trustee. māl-dhanī, māl-vālā , A. H. n. m. A proprietor. māl-zādah , n. m. A whore son. (An abuse). māl-zādī , n. f. A bawd; whore. Tum māl-mast ho gaë mere jahez se, Khāne ko māl-zādiyoṅ kī gāliyāṅ chale . Wom Rāhat. māl-sāyar . Duties; miscellaneous revenue from customs and other sources, exclusive of land. māl-sharākat . Joint, undivided property; joint stock. māl-zāmin , n. m. Opp. of hāzir-zāmin , q. v. Surety (the person); a surety for the payment of a demand against the bailee. māl-zāminī ; n. f. Opp. of hāzir-zāminī , Pecuniary bail; security in property. māl-zāmnī dākhil k. , v. a. To give security. māl-zabtī , n. f. Attached property; escheat. māl-i-ganīmat , Plunder. māl gair-manqūla ; H. aṭal dhan , n. m. Real or immoveable property. māl farod . Bonded goods; warehouse goods. māl kā band o bast , n. m. Settlement of the revenue. māl kaṭnā , v. n. 1. To be stolen. 2. To be sold in large quantities. Dillī kī manḍī meṅ baṛā māl kaṭtā haī. māl kī rasīd jo jahāz par chaṛhā hai . Bill of lading ( bilṭī ). māl-guzār , n. m. A manager of cultivation and village matters; a landlord; one who pays revenue to Govt. ( lambardār ). māl matrūkā, yā maurūsah . A bequest; legacy. māl hazam kar-ke muflis bannā, māl mār-ke divālā nikālnā . Fraudulent insolvency. māl-guzārī , n. f. The Government demand, or revenue; the land-tax. māl guzārī talab k. , v. a. To claim revenue. māl-i-lā-vāris , n. m. Unclaimed property; escheat. māl-i-lā-vāris jo zabtī meṅ āve . Escheat. māl-mārū , A. H. adj. One who embezzles. māl mārnā , v. a. 1. To fleece; swindle; abstract; make off with; sweep; strip; gut; make away with one's property. Kisī kā māl māro jo pūrā paṛe . 2. To embezzle ( khayānat k .). 3. ( Fac .) To enjoy at another's expense. māl mujrim ; H. chor ḍhor , A criminal with property. māl-i-mahmūlah , G. G. Goods on board; cargo. māl-i-mardum-khor , n. m. A swindler; one who embezzles. māl-mast , adj. Purse-proud; intoxicated with wealth. māl-mastī , n. f. Pride of wealth. māl-i-muft , n. m. Property acquired without cost or labor. Māl-i-muft, dil-i-be-rahm! Prov. Wealth gratis, a cruel heart. (It is squandered without remorse). māl-i-maqrūqah . Distrained or attached property. māl-i-manqūlah ; H. uṭhāū dhan , n. m. Personal or moveable property. māl-i-vaqf . Property devoted to religious purposes. mutaalliq māl-guzārī . G. G. Fiscal. murde kā māl . n. f. 1. Unclaimed property. 2. Anything bought cheap or got for nothing. naqd-māl , n. m. 1. A valuable prize. 2. A dainty; tit bit; delicacy.
Maldives (an island country in the Indian Ocean, South Asia; capital: Malé)
wety: Compare Bengali মালদ্বীপ (maldip) and Hindi मालदीव (māldīv).

def: alternative form of مالدیپ (māldīp): Maldives (an archipelago and country in South Asia, located in the Indian Ocean off the coast of India)
Moldova (a country in Eastern Europe)
wety: Borrowed from English Moldova.

A barbarian or one speaking any language but Sanskrit or Hindi and not observing Hindū rites. 3. An infidel. 4. (Wom.) A glutton. malechh bhāshā , A Hindi jargon, containing a large admixture of Persian and Arabic.
adj. See s.v. malā (perf. part. of malnā). s.m. Name of a district lying west and south-west of Bengal; — name of a barbarous tribe of people; a man of that tribe; — an epithet of Vishṇu.
more: adj. Aged above ten years (a horse).

def: s.m. The country of Malabār (in the Dekkan).

ety: S. मलय+वाटः, q.v.s.v. malaya
def: s.m. The country of Malabār (in the Dekkan).

ety: pass. part. of لفّ 'to involve, to wrap,' &c.
def: part. adj. Involved, wrapt up, enveloped, enclosed; folded (as a letter); — collected, gathered; — an enclosure (in a letter, &c.); an envelope; — anything wrapped up or folded (as a cabbage, &c.).
more: adj. لف wrapping. Wrapped; enveloped; enclosed.
(part. pass. of لفّ Chald. and Aram. ) adj. 1. Wrapped up, involved, enclosed; the enclosure in a letter, &c. 2. Collected, gathered.

ety: pass. part. of لفظ 'to throw out; to utter,' &c.
def: s.m. That which is spoken or uttered (by); saying; saying of a saint or a spiritual guide; — annals.
more: n. m. لفظ Pl. malfūzāt . The saying of a saint or spiritual guide.
(part. pass. of لفظ) s. Thing spoken or expressed, annals.

def: s.m. pl. (of malfūz̤), Utterances sayings; — annals.

ety: S. मल+गञ्ज+कं; cf. argajā = S. अष्ट+गंज+कं
def: adj. Dirty, filthy, foul; soiled; — rumpled, ruffled; — black and blue; — (dialec.) brackish.
more: adj. 1. Ruffled; rumpled. 2. Soiled; not clean. Badan par jo uske the kapṛe saje, Woh ujle na maile, magar malgaje .
adj. (dakh.) 1. Black and blue. 2. Brackish. ( Harris ).

def: s. (dakh.) An almshouse. ( Harris ).

ety: for malgobā = mal, 'dirt, &c.'+gobā = gowā, fr. Prk. गोवे(इ) = S. गोपय(ति), 'to secrete,' caus. of rt. गुप्
def: s.m. Entrails; garbage; — matter (from a sore); — anything that emits saliva or viscous matter; — a thick infusion; — a kind of dish, māsh or pulse mixed with dahī, q.v.; — dirt, sweepings, rubbish.
more: n. m. 1. Entrails; guts, etc. Is jānvar meṅ se baṛā malgobā niklā . 2. Matter from a sore; pus. 3. Sweepings; rubbish. 4. A thick infusion. 5. A pulse mixed with dahī , q. v.
(corrupt. of ملگوبا) s. m. 1. Emitting saliva. 2. Name of a dish ( māsh mixed with dahī ).

def: s.m. See ملغوبا malg̠obā.
s.m. = P مالگذاري māl-guẕārī, s.f. See s.v. māl, 'wealth.'
more: s. m. A tenant, a subject, a landholder, one paying rent or taxes.

def: s.f. = P مالگذار māl-guẕār, s.m. See s.v. māl, 'wealth.'
more: s. f. Rent paid in money, the paying of rent or taxes.

def: s.m. corr. of marham, q.v. s.f. Dressing or binding up (a wound); applying salve; — repair, renovation
more: (corrupt of مرهم) s. m. Salve, plaster.
subs: (-kī), To dress or bind up (a wound), to apply salve (to); — to repair, put in order, set right
s.m. One who applies dressing or salve (to a wound), dresser (of a wound).

def: s.f. (dialec.) = mallār or mallārī, q.v.
subs: s.m. The sound of the mallār.

ety: pass. part. of لحظ 'to look through half-shut eyes,' &c.
def: part. adj. Regarded, considered, contemplated; looked at kindly, viewed with affection, regard, or respect
more: adj. لحظه glanced at. Considered; contemplated; regarded. malhūz rakhnā , v. a. To regard; mind; consider.
(part. pass. of لحظ Heb. ) adj. Contemplated, considered.
subs: v.t. To keep in regard; to bear in mind; to consider, &c.

ety: Prk. मालिओ; S. मालिकः (मालिन्+कः)
def: s.m. The caste of gardeners; a man of that caste, a gardener, a florist.
more: adj. 1. Belonging to property. 2. Belonging to revenue; financial. mālī peshkār , n. m. A revenue accountant. mālī kām , n. m. Revenue matters. hukkām-i-mālī . Revenue officers.

n. m. mālan ; Poet. mālanyā , n. f. S. मालिक; Pālī, maliko . A gardener; also the caste. Mālī chāhe barasnā, dhobī chāhe dhūp, Sāh chāhe bolnā, chor chāhe chūp . The gardener wants the fruitful shower, The laundress asks a sunny hour, The banker likes to parley long, The thief prefers a silent tongue.
adj. Of property, of wealth or merchandise: civil (in oppos. to military).

def: adj. (f. -inī) = malin, q.v. s.f. The wife of a mālī or gardener; — a female gardener or flower-seller.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀮𑁆𑀮𑀺𑀆 (malliā), from Sanskrit मल्लिका (mallikā).

def: spoils of war; booty; plunder-loot
wety: From مَال (māl, “goods”) + ِ (-i, “of”) + غَنِیمَت (ġanīmat, “loot; plunder”).

ety: rel. n. fr. māl
def: adj. Of or belonging to property, or wealth, or merchandise, &c.; wealthy; — of or belonging to the revenue; financial; civil (as opp. to military)
adj. & s.m. = mleććh, q.v.
more: adj. An unclean race, those who make no distinction between clean and unclean food, a barbarian or one speaking any language but Sanskrit and not subject to the usual Hindū institutions.

ety: perf. part. of mālīdan, and = Zend marsta, rt. mared; S. मर्दित or मृदित, rt. मृद्; cf. māl
def: part. Rubbed; crushed, &c.; — s.m. Bread rubbed into crumbs, and then mixed with sugar and butter, and again rubbed well together with the hands; a cake made of bread so rubbed, or of pounded meal mixed with butter and sugar; — a fine woollen cloth (made of Kashmīr lamb's wool).
more: (from ماليدن) s. m. Bread or cakes made with flour, milk, butter, sugar, &c. (or, wheaten cakes, dried, pounded and mixed up with ghī and sugar. Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.m. corr. of next, q.v.

ety: contrac. of mālīda, perf. part. of māl-īdan, 'to rub,' &c.
def: s.m. A cake made of pounded meal (or of flour), milk, butter, and sugar; — a kind of fine woollen cloth (made of Kashmīr lamb's-wool).
more: n. m. P. mālīdan to rub. 1. Pounded meal cakes mixed with butter, and sugar. 2. A fine woollen cloth, made of Kashmīr lamb's wool. malīdah bāṅdhe phirnā , Slang, v. n. To be willing to prostitute oneself (as a catamite).

ety: v.n. fr. ملح 'to salt,' &c.; see milḥ
def: adj. Beautiful; agreeable, sweet, charming, piquant; of dark-brown colour, nut-brown (syn. sāṅʼolā).
more: (from ملح Heb. Chald. and Aram. ) adj. Agreeable, sweet, charming, beautiful.

ety: act. part. of ملك 'to possess,' &c.
def: s.m. Possessor, proprietor, occupier, owner, master, employer, lord, ruler; the Supreme Being; — a husband
more: n. m. ملک possessing. See صاحب (4, 6). 1. Owner; proprietor. Sher ke mālak bāṅṛā siyār! Bhoj. Prov. A jackal without a tail, the tiger's lord! 2. Master; lord. 3. God. Mālik the wo to āp unheṅ bālpan se kyā? Wāṅ bāl-pan, javānī, buṛhāpā sab ek thā . Krishn Nazīr. He was the Lord, childhood was naught to him. Childhood, and youth, and age alike to him. 4. A husband. 5. One empowered; an employer. mālik arāzī , n. m. A landholder. mālik-i-ālā . A superior proprietor. mālik-ul-mulk , n. m. lit . lord of the country. A title addressed to sovereigns, princes, prime ministers, etc. mālik aur kar āëdār , n. m. Landlord and tenant. mālik-i-hissa . A shareholder. mālik haqīqī . Proprietor de facto ; the real owner. mālik-i-deh . A village proprietor. mālik k. , v. a. To empower; give a claim to; entitle. mālik-i-makān, yā zamīn , Householder; landlord. mālik-i-hunḍī , n. m. The holder of a bill o…
(part. act. of ملک Heb. ) s. m. Master, lord, possessor, proprietor, owner. (Pers. plur. مالکان). مالک الحزن māliku-l-ḥazin or مالک الحزين māliku-l-ḥazīn, Name of an aquatick bird.
subs: s.m. Holder of a share or shares, shareholder
s.m. The real owner; the proprietor de facto
v.t. To make (one) the proprietor or owner (of), &c.; to empower, entitle, give a claim (to)
s.m. A kind of water-fowl, a bittern (?)
s m. 'Lord of the territory or kingdom,' a title addressed to sovereigns, princes, prime-ministers, &c.
s.m. Holder of a bill or draft
(-kā), To be or become the owner or proprietor (of), to own, to have a title (to).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مالک (mālik), from Arabic مَالِك (mālik, “owner”).

def: s.m. A garland-maker, a gardener, florist, flower-gatherer
